headlines.headline: Saudi Oil Exports Surge 37% as Kingdom Bypasses Hormuz via Red Sea Route
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia reported a 37.4% surge in oil exports in March, the highest level in 3.5 years, as the East-West pipeline allowed crude shipments to bypass the Strait of Hormuz disruptions and reach global markets through the Red Sea port of Yanbu.

headlines.headline: Turkey Opposition Vows Resistance as Court Ousts CHP Leader in Unprecedented Ruling
headlines.summary: Turkey's opposition vowed to fight a court ruling that ousted CHP leader Ozgur Ozel and annulled the party's congress, calling it a 'judicial coup' aimed at extending President Erdogan's 23-year rule. The lira hit a record low as political crisis deepens.

headlines.headline: Venezuela Surpasses Saudi Arabia to Become India's Third-Largest Crude Supplier
headlines.summary: Venezuela shipped 417,000 barrels per day to India in May, overtaking Saudi Arabia and the US as India's third-largest crude supplier, as Indian refiners pivoted away from Middle Eastern sources disrupted by the Strait of Hormuz crisis.

headlines.headline: Taliban Decree Formalizes Child Marriage Rules, UN Expresses Grave Concern
headlines.summary: The UN warned that a new Taliban decree on marriage separation implies child marriage is permitted, saying girls' silence at puberty can be interpreted as consent. The Afghan justice ministry defended the decree as following Islamic law.

headlines.headline: UAE Official Sees 50-50 Chance for Hormuz Deal, Warns Iran Against Overplaying Hand
headlines.summary: UAE presidential adviser Anwar Gargash estimated only a 50-50 chance of a US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, warning Tehran against overestimating its leverage in negotiations as Gulf states face mounting economic pressure.

headlines.headline: Pakistan Mediates US-Iran Peace Talks Amid Uranium and Hormuz Disputes
headlines.summary: Iran foreign minister met with Pakistani officials to discuss proposals for ending the US-Iran war, as both nations remain at odds over uranium stockpile and Strait of Hormuz control.

headlines.headline: US-China Thaw Plants Seeds for Taiwan Flashpoint
headlines.summary: Trump says pending 14 billion dollar Taiwan arms deal is a very good negotiating chip with Beijing, even as Xi ramps up military and diplomatic pressure on Taipei.

headlines.headline: NATO Allies Confused by Trump Sudden Shift on European Troop Deployments
headlines.summary: NATO allies and defense officials expressed bewilderment at Trump announcement about potential US troop movements in Europe, creating uncertainty about American commitment to the alliance.

headlines.headline: Taiwan denies receiving US military sales changes notification
headlines.summary: Taiwan presidential office says no information about US adjusting military sales after acting US Navy Secretary suggests pause due to Iran war needs.

headlines.headline: Belarus pledges not to join Ukraine war but will defend with Russia
headlines.summary: President Lukashenko says Belarus will not be dragged into Ukraine conflict but will jointly defend against aggression with Russia after nuclear drills end.

headlines.headline: Four killed in Ukrainian drone attack on Luhansk student dormitory
headlines.summary: Russian officials report four dead and 35 children wounded in overnight Ukrainian drone strike on student dormitory in Russian-controlled Luhansk region.

headlines.headline: UK police say Prince Andrew investigation will be lengthy
headlines.summary: British police say investigation into King Charles brother Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor will be long and complex following arrest on misconduct allegations.

headlines.headline: Irish PM to discuss Middle East conflict with Pope
headlines.summary: Taoiseach Micheal Martin will discuss ongoing Middle East conflict and reconciliation process in Ireland during meeting with Pope.

headlines.headline: US approves $108 million HAWK missile system sale to Ukraine
headlines.summary: The US State Department approved a $108 million sale of HAWK missile system equipment to Ukraine, including maintenance support, spare parts, and logistics assistance for the air defense systems.

perspectives.angle: US Approves $108M Hawk Missile System Sale to Ukraine
perspectives.summary: The US State Department approved a potential $108.1 million foreign military sale to Ukraine for Hawk missile system maintenance, spare parts and logistics services, bolstering Kyiv's air defense capabilities.

headlines.headline: Ukraine foreign minister meets new Hungarian counterpart on minority rights
headlines.summary: Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha held his first meeting with Hungary's new Foreign Minister Anita Orban in Sweden to discuss minority rights and bilateral relations between the two countries.

headlines.headline: Hajj pilgrimage overshadowed by Middle East war tensions
headlines.summary: Over a million Muslims gathered in Mecca for the hajj pilgrimage under the shadow of the ongoing Middle East conflict, with Saudi officials attempting to keep the war from visitors' minds following Iranian attacks on Gulf targets.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Strikes Russian Oil Refinery 700 km from Border
headlines.summary: President Zelenskiy announced Ukrainian forces attacked a Russian oil refinery in Yaroslavl, marking another long-range strike on Russian energy infrastructure as the war continues.

headlines.headline: Turkey Political Crisis Deepens as Opposition Vows Resistance
headlines.summary: Turkeys opposition vowed to resist a court ruling that ousted its leader and annulled its congress, a move critics say aims to prolong President Erdogans 23-year rule.

headlines.headline: Oil Prices Rise as Investors Doubt US-Iran Breakthrough
headlines.summary: Oil prices climbed as investors remained skeptical about prospects for a breakthrough in US-Iran peace talks, with Brent crude holding elevated amid Hormuz shipping disruptions.

perspectives.angle: US Oil Prices Rise as Investors Doubt Breakthrough in Iran Peace Talks
perspectives.summary: U.S. crude futures rose over $1 as investors doubt prospects for a breakthrough in U.S.-Iran peace talks, with both sides stuck on uranium stockpile and Strait of Hormuz controls.

headlines.headline: China Cuts Oil Imports as Higher US Exports Wrongfoot Market
headlines.summary: Chinese oil imports have fallen while US exports increased, catching market bulls off guard as physical crude prices hit all-time highs above $160 per barrel amid the Iran crisis.

headlines.headline: Euro Zone Growth Set to Slow in 2026 as Middle East War Fuels Inflation
headlines.summary: The European Commission warned that euro zone growth will slow in 2026 as the Middle East conflict triggers the second energy shock in less than five years.

perspectives.angle: Euro Zone Growth to Slow in 2026 as Middle East Conflict Fuels Inflation
perspectives.summary: The European Commission forecasts economic slowdown in 2026 as the Middle East conflict triggers the second energy shock in five years. Impact severity depends on conflict duration.

perspectives.angle: Japan Auto Exports to Middle East Nearly Wiped Out by War
perspectives.summary: Japanese vehicle exports to the Middle East collapsed in April as the US-Israeli war on Iran disrupted shipping routes critical for automakers like Toyota and Nissan.

headlines.headline: Kremlin Denies China Trained Russian Soldiers for Ukraine War
headlines.summary: The Kremlin dismissed as false a Reuters report that Chinas army secretly trained about 200 Russian soldiers in China late last year, some of whom went on to fight in Ukraine. The denial comes amid scrutiny of China-Russia military cooperation.

perspectives.angle: Kremlin Dismisses Report China Trained Russian Soldiers for Ukraine War
perspectives.summary: The Kremlin called false a Reuters report that China secretly trained about 200 Russian soldiers in China late last year, some of whom went on to fight in Ukraine.

headlines.headline: Russian Refinery Partially Shuts After Ukrainian Drone Strike
headlines.summary: NORSI, Russias fourth-largest oil refinery, shut its main primary processing unit after a Ukrainian drone attack. The incident adds to a series of strikes on Russian energy infrastructure.

perspectives.angle: Russian NORSI Refinery Partially Shut After Ukrainian Drone Attack
perspectives.summary: NORSI, Russia's fourth-largest oil refinery, shut its main primary CDU-6 unit after a Ukrainian drone attack on Wednesday, according to industry sources.

headlines.headline: Trump Vows US Will Retrieve Irans Enriched Uranium
headlines.summary: President Trump declared the United States will eventually recover Irans stockpile of highly enriched uranium, despite Tehran refusing to hand over the material. The statement underscores continued tensions over nuclear negotiations.

perspectives.angle: Trump vows US will retrieve Irans enriched uranium despite rejection
perspectives.summary: President Donald Trump pledged the United States will eventually recover Irans stockpile of highly enriched uranium, countering Tehrans refusal to hand over the material. The standoff remains a key obstacle in peace talks.

headlines.headline: Exxon in Talks to Return to Venezuela After Two-Decade Absence
headlines.summary: Exxon Mobil is negotiating to acquire oil production rights in Venezuela, nearly 20 years after being expelled from the country. The potential deal reflects shifting dynamics in global energy markets.

perspectives.angle: Exxon Negotiates Return to Venezuela in Major Policy Reversal
perspectives.summary: US oil giant Exxon is in talks to resume operations in Venezuela, ending a years-long standoff and marking a significant shift in energy diplomacy under the Trump administration.

headlines.headline: Ireland to Introduce Bill Curbing Trade With Israeli Settlements
headlines.summary: Irelands foreign minister announced a bill to restrict trade with Israeli settlements in the occupied West Bank will be introduced to parliament in coming weeks. The move follows growing international pressure over settlement expansion.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Denies Knowledge of US Arms Pause Despite Navy Secretary Statement
headlines.summary: Taiwan presidential office says it has not received any notification of changes to military sales after Acting Navy Secretary Hung Cao said the US was pausing a $14 billion arms package to ensure munitions for the Iran operation.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Leaked Secret UAE Wartime Visit to Boost Domestic Standing
headlines.summary: Analysis reveals Israeli PM Netanyahu deliberately disclosed his clandestine March meeting with UAE President MBZ to portray himself as a regional statesman ahead of elections, drawing Emirati anger and potentially undermining Gulf balancing act.

headlines.headline: Injured Palestinian Girls Arrive in Boston for Medical Treatment
headlines.summary: Three Palestinian children from Gaza, including a double amputee and burn victims, arrived in the US for urgent medical care as part of HEAL Palestine evacuation efforts, highlighting Gaza healthcare collapse.

headlines.headline: UK Refuses to Revoke Hong Kong Trade Office Privileges After Spy Case
headlines.summary: British government says Hong Kong Economic and Trade Office can continue legitimate activity despite conviction of two men linked to the office for spying on activists, resisting political pressure to revoke diplomatic privileges.

headlines.headline: Pakistan mediates US-Iran peace talks as uranium dispute persists
headlines.summary: Iran's foreign minister met his Pakistani counterpart to discuss proposals to end the US-Israeli war. Tehran and Washington remain at odds over uranium stockpile and Strait of Hormuz controls.

perspectives.angle: Supreme Leader orders Iran keep enriched uranium, hardening stance in talks
perspectives.summary: Ayatollah Khamenei issued a directive that Irans near-weapons-grade uranium should not be sent abroad, according to senior Iranian sources. This directly counters a key US demand in ongoing peace negotiations.

perspectives.angle: Pakistan Mediates as Iran Hardens Stance on Uranium in Peace Talks
perspectives.summary: Pakistan intensified diplomacy to advance US-Iran peace negotiations, but Tehran appeared to harden its position on nuclear materials. Trump warned of military action if he does not get the right answers, while Iran reviews the latest US proposal.

headlines.headline: Quad foreign ministers to meet in Delhi amid China tensions
headlines.summary: The US, Japan, Australia and India will hold a meeting of Quad foreign ministers in New Delhi on May 26, Japan's foreign ministry announced.

headlines.headline: Turkish court ousts main opposition leader Ozel
headlines.summary: A Turkish court annulled the 2023 party congress that elected opposition CHP leader Ozgur Ozel, effectively removing him from his position and dealing a blow to President Erdogan's challengers.

perspectives.angle: Turkish court annuls main opposition party congress in blow to CHP
perspectives.summary: An Ankara court suspended the leadership of Turkey's main opposition CHP, which defeated Erdogan's AKP in 2024 local elections. The ruling removes the party that won major victories in Istanbul and Ankara from power.

headlines.headline: China's Hengli petrochemical empire faces US sanctions chill
headlines.summary: The Hengli Group, one of China's largest private oil refiners, has been thrust into the center of a geopolitical power struggle as US sanctions target the company.

headlines.headline: APEC trade envoys gather in China to discuss supply chain resilience
headlines.summary: Asia-Pacific trade envoys met in China to discuss multilateral cooperation and supply chain resilience amid global shocks including the US-Iranian conflict.

headlines.headline: Trump announces additional 5,000 US troops for Poland
headlines.summary: President Trump declared the US will send 5,000 additional troops to Poland, reversing recent administration moves to reduce European deployments. The announcement stirred confusion amid contradictory statements about US troop presence in Europe.

headlines.headline: Belarus will not be dragged into Ukraine war, Lukashenko says
headlines.summary: Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko dismissed any notion Belarus would join Russia in the Ukraine war, but stated Belarus and Russia would jointly defend themselves if attacked. The statement comes amid ongoing regional tensions.

perspectives.angle: Belarus Says It Wont Be Dragged Into Ukraine War
perspectives.summary: Belarusian President Lukashenko dismissed any notion that Belarus would be drawn into the Ukraine conflict, while affirming that Russia and Belarus would jointly defend themselves if attacked.

headlines.headline: Latvia and Lithuania issue drone alerts, NATO jets scramble
headlines.summary: Lithuania and Latvia detected drones in their airspace and urged residents to seek shelter while NATO fighter jets scrambled to intercept. This marks the latest in a series of security incidents in the Baltic states.

perspectives.angle: Latvia and Lithuania Issue Drone Alerts, Scramble NATO Jets
perspectives.summary: Baltic states detected drones in their airspace and scrambled NATO fighter jets, the latest in a series of security incidents involving Ukrainian drones targeting Russian oil exports.

headlines.headline: US House Republicans cancel Iran war powers vote
headlines.summary: House Republicans cancelled a planned vote on Iran war powers, reflecting internal divisions over congressional authorization for military action. The move comes amid ongoing US-Iran negotiations.

perspectives.angle: US House Republicans Cancel Iran War Powers Vote
perspectives.summary: Republican leaders unexpectedly canceled a House vote on a resolution to end the Iran war without congressional authorization, days after a similar measure advanced in the Senate.

headlines.headline: US sanctions nine Hezbollah-aligned individuals in Lebanon
headlines.summary: The United States imposed sanctions on nine individuals with alleged Hezbollah connections in Lebanon, continuing pressure on the Iran-backed group amid broader regional conflict.

perspectives.angle: US Sanctions Nine Hezbollah-Linked Individuals in Lebanon
perspectives.summary: The US announced sanctions against nine individuals, including Iran's designated ambassador to Lebanon, for obstructing Lebanon's peace process and impeding Hezbollah disarmament.

headlines.headline: Trump Postpones AI Executive Order Citing China Competition
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump postponed signing an executive order on artificial intelligence, stating he did not like certain aspects and did not want to take steps that might undermine the US position in its AI competition with China.

headlines.headline: Turkish Opposition Leader Vows to Stay After Court Ousts Him
headlines.summary: Turkey's main opposition CHP leader Ozgur Ozel vowed not to leave party headquarters after a court ruling removed him from his position, deepening the country's political crisis ahead of crucial elections.

perspectives.angle: Turkish Court Annuls Main Opposition CHP Congress in Major Political Blow
perspectives.summary: An appeals court ruled that the Republican Peoples Party 2023 congress is legally invalid, dealing a significant setback to Turkeys main opposition party and deepening the countrys political uncertainty.

headlines.headline: Iran Claims Military Control Over Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Iran published a map claiming "armed forces oversight" across 22,000 sq km of the strategic waterway, extending into UAE and Omani waters. The UAE rejected the claims as "fragments of dreams," while the US directed 94 ships to alter course since the blockade began.

headlines.headline: Greenlanders Protest Outside New US Consulate in Nuuk
headlines.summary: Hundreds gathered in the capital chanting "Greenland is for Greenlanders" as the US opened a new consulate amid Trump's push for control over the territory. Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen boycotted the opening ceremony.

headlines.headline: Alberta to Hold Referendum on Separating from Canada
headlines.summary: The oil-rich province will vote October 19 on whether to remain in Canada or begin a binding independence process. Premier Danielle Smith said she will vote to stay, but separatists gathered over 300,000 signatures demanding the vote.

headlines.headline: Rubio Declares Cuba National Security Threat After Castro Indictment
headlines.summary: The US Secretary of State called Cuba a "leading sponsor of terrorism" and said diplomatic resolution was unlikely. Cuba's foreign minister accused Rubio of trying to "instigate military aggression" as tensions escalate following the indictment of former President Raúl Castro.

perspectives.angle: US Designates Cuba as National Security Threat as Rubio Accuses Castro Regime
perspectives.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio declared Cuba a national security threat, with Cuba's foreign minister accusing the US of trying to "instigate a military aggression" amid heightened tensions following the indictment of Raúl Castro.

headlines.headline: Israel Deports All Foreign Activists from Gaza Flotilla
headlines.summary: Israel deported all foreign activists seized from a Gaza-bound aid flotilla after Israeli forces intercepted the vessel. The move follows international condemnation after an Israeli minister mocked the detained activists.

perspectives.angle: Israel Deports 430 Foreign Flotilla Activists After Ben Gvir Controversy
perspectives.summary: Israel has deported all 430 foreign activists from the Gaza-bound flotilla, with Turkey sending planes to collect them. Italy has requested EU sanctions on far-right minister Itamar Ben Gvir over his handling of detained activists, while Poland considers banning him.

headlines.headline: Czech President Warns Europe: Ukraine Is Not a Charity Case
headlines.summary: Czech President Petr Pavel opened GLOBSEC 2026 with a warning against a "bad peace" for Ukraine and called for European procurement reform, arguing that Ukraine serves as Europe's defense laboratory.

headlines.headline: Rubio to confront NATO allies over lack of support in Iran war
headlines.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will meet NATO allies for the first time since President Trump questioned the alliance over divisions on the Iran war. The meeting comes amid troop drawdowns from Europe.

headlines.headline: Reuters investigation: Trumps diplomacy unravels as allies forge own paths
headlines.summary: Donald Trumps threats, personal envoys and hollowed-out embassies are reshaping US diplomacy. Allies from Europe to Asia are ignoring presidential rhetoric and building independent diplomatic channels.

perspectives.angle: Trump Diplomacy Unraveling as Allies Forge New Channels
perspectives.summary: Investigation reveals how Trump's threats, personal envoys and hollowed-out embassies are reshaping US diplomacy. Allies across Europe and Asia are ignoring presidential rhetoric and creating alternative diplomatic pathways.

headlines.headline: Asian currencies hit record lows as oil shock hits region
headlines.summary: Asias policymakers are taking urgent steps to shore up economies on the front line of the global energy supply shock. Currencies are falling to record lows and pressure is forcing up interest rates.

headlines.headline: Israel launches new AI battlefield operations division
headlines.summary: The IDF announced the creation of "Alumot," a new division integrating AI into battlefield operations, combining combat soldiers with technology professionals.

headlines.headline: SpaceX postpones Starship megarocket test launch
headlines.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX called off the highly anticipated test launch of its upgraded Starship megarocket, postponing the demonstration without specifying a new date.

headlines.headline: Trump triumphal arch approved by ally-stacked commission
headlines.summary: An arts commission filled with Trump allies approved designs for his massive triumphal arch project, despite ongoing lawsuits seeking to halt construction.

headlines.headline: Singapore deploys AI robots for cleaning, patrol and delivery
headlines.summary: Robots are being rolled out in Singapore's AI zone to handle cleaning, security patrol, and goods delivery, with analysts emphasizing safety priorities for workplace integration.

headlines.headline: Taiwan President Says Ready to Talk With Trump
headlines.summary: Taiwan President Lai Ching-te would be happy to speak with US President Donald Trump, Taiwan’s foreign ministry said, in what would be an unprecedented call between the US and Taiwanese leaders.

perspectives.angle: China May React Badly to Any Trump-Taiwan President Call
perspectives.summary: US President Donald Trump said he would speak with Taiwan's President Lai Ching-te, an unprecedented move that could escalate tensions with Beijing. China considers Taiwan a breakaway province and opposes any official contact between Taiwan and foreign leaders.

perspectives.angle: Trump to Speak With Taiwan Leader About Arms Deal, Risking China Tensions
perspectives.summary: President Trump said he would talk to Taiwan's president about a possible weapons sale to the self-governing democracy. Direct dialogue would anger China, which claims Taiwan as its territory and opposes contact between Taiwanese and foreign leaders.

headlines.headline: US to Send 5,000 Additional Troops to Poland Amid NATO Tensions
headlines.summary: President Trump announced the deployment of 5,000 additional US troops to Poland, following weeks of conflicting statements about American military presence in Europe.

perspectives.angle: Trump reverses course, announces 5,000 additional troops for Poland
perspectives.summary: A week after canceling a US Army deployment to Poland, Trump announced 5,000 additional troops will be sent to bolster NATO's eastern flank.

headlines.headline: Germany Proposes Associate EU Membership for Ukraine
headlines.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz proposed giving Ukraine a direct role in EU structures as an interim step toward full membership, potentially facilitating a deal to end the war with Russia.

perspectives.angle: Germany's Merz Proposes Associate EU Membership for Ukraine
perspectives.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz wants the EU to offer Ukraine "associate membership" as an interim step toward full membership, potentially facilitating a deal to end the war with Russia.

headlines.headline: Russia Launches Nuclear-Capable Missiles in Drills Amid NATO Tensions
headlines.summary: Russia fired nuclear-capable missiles and issued nuclear munitions to some units as part of major nuclear exercises amid heightened tensions with NATO over the Ukraine war and drone activity in the Baltic region.

perspectives.angle: Russia Conducts Major Nuclear Drills Amid Ongoing Tensions
perspectives.summary: Russia began three days of nuclear drills including ballistic and cruise missile launches, the defense ministry announced, as Moscow continues its confrontation with the West over Ukraine.

headlines.headline: French Economic Activity Contracts at Fastest Pace in 5.5 Years
headlines.summary: France's private sector economy contracted in May at its sharpest pace in five-and-a-half years, with accelerated decline in services and fresh drop in manufacturing.

perspectives.angle: French Economy Contracts at Fastest Pace in 5 Years
perspectives.summary: France's private sector shrank at its steepest rate in five-and-a-half years in May, with services and manufacturing both declining as the Iran war energy shock hit Europe hard.

headlines.headline: Iran Rebuilding Military Industrial Base Faster Than Expected
headlines.summary: Iran has already restarted some drone production during the six-week ceasefire, rebuilding its military industrial base faster than anticipated, CNN reports citing U.S. intelligence assessments.

perspectives.angle: Iran rebuilding military industrial base faster than expected
perspectives.summary: Iran has restarted some drone production during the six-week ceasefire, with US intelligence assessments indicating faster-than-expected military industrial base reconstruction.

headlines.headline: China’s Geopolitical Weight on Display as Xi Hosts Trump and Putin
headlines.summary: Back-to-back visits by Trump and Putin to Beijing highlight Chinese power, raising questions about whether Xi will help end the Iran and Ukraine wars.

headlines.headline: US Official Says Canada Yet to Prove Itself as Credible Defense Partner
headlines.summary: A US official criticized Canada’s defense spending and NATO commitments days after the Pentagon paused a joint military cooperation advisory board for reassessment.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Needs New Communications Model to Counter Russian Influence, Budanov Says
headlines.summary: Ukrainian military intelligence chief Kyrylo Budanov warns Russia uses "human consciousness engineering" on civilians under occupation, requiring new countermeasures.

headlines.headline: China’s Memory Chip Champion YMTC Charts IPO Path Amid Tech Self-Sufficiency Push
headlines.summary: YMTC and CXMT progress toward IPOs as Beijing pushes tech self-sufficiency, highlighting China’s efforts to build domestic semiconductor capabilities.

headlines.headline: Iran Executes Two Under Security Charges
headlines.summary: Iran executed two individuals on charges of forming a group to disrupt national security and membership in a terrorist organization, according to Tasnim news agency.

headlines.headline: India Weighs Options Including Rate Hike as Rupee Slides
headlines.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is considering all available options to stabilize the rupee, including an interest rate hike, amid ongoing currency pressures.

headlines.headline: Poland Seeks Ban on Israeli Minister Ben-Gvir Over Flotilla Detentions
headlines.summary: Poland requested a ban on Israel far-right police minister Itamar Ben-Gvir from entering the country following outrage over the detention of Gaza flotilla activists.

perspectives.angle: Poland Seeks Ban on Israel's Ben-Gvir After Gaza Flotilla Activists Detained
perspectives.summary: Poland's foreign ministry requested a ban on Israeli far-right minister Itamar Ben-Gvir from entering the country following outrage over the detention of Gaza flotilla activists. Warsaw summoned Israel's envoy demanding release and apology.

headlines.headline: Georgia Jails Opposition Leader for 2.5 Years
headlines.summary: A Georgian court sentenced a senior opposition figure to 2.5 years in prison on charges of sabotage and inciting a coup during last year local elections.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo Plans Visit to Uruguay in November
headlines.summary: Pope Leo is scheduled to visit Uruguay in November as part of a regional tour, according to local Catholic Church sources.

headlines.headline: Air France and Airbus Found Guilty of Manslaughter Over 2009 Plane Crash
headlines.summary: A French court found both companies guilty over the Air France Flight 447 disaster that killed 228 people when an Airbus A330 crashed into the Atlantic Ocean en route from Rio to Paris.

headlines.headline: Inside Iran's Revolutionary Guards: The Hard-Line Military Circle Running the Country
headlines.summary: Decision making in Iran is now guided by a small group of men associated with the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps, as the country navigates its confrontation with the US and regional adversaries.

headlines.headline: Japan Opens Independent NATO Mission as Regional Tensions Rise
headlines.summary: Japan formally inaugurated an independent mission to NATO, separating from its Belgian embassy, as Tokyo deepens cooperation with the alliance amid escalating tensions with Russia, China, and North Korea.

headlines.headline: European Parliament Votes Overwhelmingly for Tougher Iran Sanctions
headlines.summary: The European Parliament adopted a resolution with 516-14 votes demanding expanded EU sanctions on Iranian officials and entities, including the IRGC, over human rights violations and use of the death penalty to suppress dissent.

headlines.headline: Singapore Airlines Expands Europe Routes to Capture Gulf Carrier Market Share
headlines.summary: Singapore Airlines is adding long-haul flights to Europe to capture premium traffic diverted from Gulf carriers amid Middle East conflict disruptions, leveraging its strong balance sheet and oil hedging strategy.

headlines.headline: US Navy Accelerates Laser Weapons Development for Future Missile Defense
headlines.summary: Chief of Naval Operations Adm. Daryl Caudle testified that directed energy weapons are critical for naval warfare, arguing lasers could free up VLS cells currently used for defensive interceptors for offensive weapons.

perspectives.angle: US Navy Accelerates Laser Weapons Fleet After Iran Conflict Success
perspectives.summary: Operation Epic Fury has strengthened the case for laser weapons, and the US Navy is now pushing to equip every ship with directed energy capabilities.

headlines.headline: Russia Launches Nuclear War Games on Belarus Border
headlines.summary: Russia conducts major nuclear military exercises in Belarus as NATO issues stark warnings. The drills are seen as an attempt to regain military initiative by threatening escalation on Europe's doorstep.

perspectives.angle: Russia launches nuclear exercises with Belarus as NATO tensions rise
perspectives.summary: Russia deployed nuclear-capable missiles and issued nuclear munitions to Belarus units in major exercises involving 64,000 personnel, amid heightened tensions over Ukraine and Baltic drone activity.

headlines.headline: Brazil Senator Bolsonaro Seeks White House Meeting Amid Campaign Crisis
headlines.summary: Senator Flavio Bolsonaro seeks meeting with President Trump as presidential hopeful attempts to distance himself from scandal-hit banker revelations.

headlines.headline: IEA Chief Warns Oil Markets Nearing Red Zone as Iran Crisis Continues
headlines.summary: International Energy Agency head warns that ongoing tensions with Iran are pushing global oil markets toward critical levels, with potential supply disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Russia and China Condemn US Over Raul Castro Indictment
headlines.summary: Moscow and Beijing criticize Washington decision to charge former Cuban leader Raul Castro with conspiracy to kill US nationals, calling it a pretext for escalation against Cuba.

headlines.headline: Top UN Court Rules Workers Right to Strike Protected
headlines.summary: International Court of Justice issues nonbinding ruling that workers right to strike is protected under key treaty, expected to influence global labor relations.

headlines.headline: Chinese Firm Breaks Ground on $5B Aviation Complex in UAE Amid Iran War
headlines.summary: A Chinese state-owned company has launched construction of a massive aviation complex in the UAE, signaling continued economic confidence in the region despite ongoing conflict risks.

headlines.headline: Chinese Container Ship Among Few Vessels Crossing Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: A Chinese-operated container ship was one of the few vessels to cross the Strait of Hormuz in the past 24 hours, as the critical waterway remains largely blocked amid the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Two Men Charged Under New Federal Deepfake Law
headlines.summary: Federal prosecutors charged two men with using AI to create nude videos and photos under a new law targeting deepfake content. This marks one of the first prosecutions under the "Take It Down" legislation.

perspectives.angle: Two Men Charged Under New US Deepfake Porn Law
perspectives.summary: Two men are among the earliest defendants to face charges under the Take It Down Act, a US law signed last year targeting AI-generated explicit content. The cases mark a significant test of new legislation addressing deepfake technology.

headlines.headline: U.S. Threatens to Revoke Palestinian UN Delegation Visas
headlines.summary: The Trump administration warned it would revoke visas for the Palestinian UN delegation if the ambassador pursues candidacy for the UN General Assembly vice presidency. The threat reflects escalating U.S. pressure on Palestinian diplomatic representation.

perspectives.angle: US Threatens to Revoke Palestinian UN Delegation Visas
perspectives.summary: A State Department cable instructed US diplomats to pressure Palestinian officials to withdraw Ambassador Riyad Mansour candidacy for UN General Assembly vice president or face visa revocation. The Trump administration previously barred Palestinian officials from attending the UN General Assembly last year.

headlines.headline: GAESA: The Military Conglomerate at Center of U.S.-Cuba Tensions
headlines.summary: A Reuters explainer on GAESA, the Cuban military-controlled business empire that has become a focal point in U.S.-Cuba tensions as Secretary Rubio blames the conglomerate for Cuba’s economic crisis.

headlines.headline: Rubio Doubtful on Cuba Diplomacy as Trump Raises Military Threat
headlines.summary: Secretary Rubio expressed skepticism about diplomatic engagement with Cuba while President Trump again raised the possibility of U.S. military intervention, following criminal charges against former Cuban leader Raúl Castro.

headlines.headline: Bank of Japan policymaker calls for rate hike citing war-driven inflation
headlines.summary: BOJ board member Junko Koeda said the central bank should raise interest rates as Middle East war price pressures may push inflation above the 2% target, strengthening the case for a June hike.

perspectives.angle: Bank of Japan Calls for Rate Hike as War-Fueled Inflation Looms
perspectives.summary: BOJ board member Junko Koeda urged interest rate hikes at an appropriate pace, warning that Middle East war price pressures could push underlying inflation above the 2 percent target. The comments bolstered expectations for a June rate increase.

perspectives.angle: Federal Reserve to Hold Rates Steady as War-Driven Inflation Persists
perspectives.summary: Most economists polled by Reuters expect the Fed to avoid cutting interest rates this year, pushing rate reduction expectations into 2026 as the Iran conflict continues to pressure global energy prices and inflation.

headlines.headline: Malaysia demands TikTok explain AI deepfake account insulting king
headlines.summary: Malaysia's government summoned TikTok over a fake account using AI to create offensive content about the king, including false claims about eating pork and images with his face on animal bodies.

headlines.headline: Thai princess Bajrakitiyabha health declines amid multiple infections
headlines.summary: The medical condition of Thai Princess Bajrakitiyabha has worsened due to multiple infections affecting several organs. The 44-year-old princess has been hospitalized since December 2022 after suffering cardiac arrest.

headlines.headline: US seeks major expansion of energy exports to India, says Rubio
headlines.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced the US wants to significantly expand energy exports to India amid the Iran crisis disrupting traditional supply routes. The move aims to strengthen US-India energy security ties.

headlines.headline: NATO jet downs suspected stray Ukrainian drone over Estonia
headlines.summary: A NATO fighter jet intercepted and shot down a suspected Ukrainian drone that strayed into Estonian airspace. The incident highlights growing risks as Ukraine escalates long-range drone strikes on Russian infrastructure.

perspectives.angle: NATO Jet Downs Stray Ukrainian Drone Over Estonia in First Such Incident
perspectives.summary: A NATO fighter jet shot down a suspected stray Ukrainian drone over Estonia, marking the first such incident. Kyiv accused Moscow of intentionally redirecting Ukrainian drones into NATO territory.

headlines.headline: US Diplomacy Unravels Under Trump, Reshaping Global Alliances
headlines.summary: A Reuters investigation finds that Trump's threats, personal envoys and hollowed-out embassies are reshaping US diplomacy. Allies from Europe to Asia are forging new channels as decisions increasingly driven by personalities, not institutions.

headlines.headline: Germany Charges Two with Iran-Backed Murder Plot Against Pro-Israel Figure
headlines.summary: German prosecutors charged a Danish national and his Afghan accomplice with planning to kill a prominent pro-Israel public figure and spying on Jews for Iranian intelligence. The case highlights Iran's continued covert operations in Europe.

headlines.headline: Ethiopia Accuses Egypt of Obstructing Red Sea Access Bid
headlines.summary: Ethiopia accused Egypt of attempting to undermine its efforts to secure Red Sea access, days after Cairo signed a maritime cooperation agreement with Eritrea. The dispute adds to regional tensions over strategic waterways and access.

headlines.headline: UK Summons Israeli Diplomat Over Gaza Flotilla Activist Treatment
headlines.summary: The UK summoned Israel's top diplomat after far-right minister Ben Gvir posted a video taunting detained pro-Palestinian activists. Hundreds of flotilla activists have been deported following international backlash over their treatment.

perspectives.angle: Israeli Minister Condemned for Taunting Detained Gaza Flotilla Activists
perspectives.summary: Far-right minister Itamar Ben-Gvir posted a video taunting handcuffed pro-Palestinian activists from a Gaza-bound aid flotilla intercepted by Israeli forces, drawing criticism from France, Italy, and even Prime Minister Netanyahu.

perspectives.angle: Multiple Nations Summon Israeli Envoys Over Flotilla Activist Treatment
perspectives.summary: France, Netherlands, Canada, Ireland, Spain, Italy, Germany, Greece, Britain, Turkey and New Zealand summoned Israeli ambassadors over National Security Minister Ben-Gvir's video showing detained Gaza flotilla activists kneeling and tied. Netanyahu criticized the minister's actions.

headlines.headline: US Supreme Court Rules for Cuban Property Owners in Castro-Era Case
headlines.summary: The Supreme Court ruled 8-1 in favor of Havana Docks, reviving claims against cruise lines that used property confiscated during the Cuban revolution. The decision allows owners of seized Cuban property to pursue compensation.

headlines.headline: Chinese Supertankers Exit Hormuz as Iran Conflict Shows Signs of Thaw
headlines.summary: Two Chinese supertankers were among a group of vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, suggesting a possible de-escalation in the US-Iran standoff that has disrupted global oil shipments.

headlines.headline: Putins Attack on Hungarians in Ukraine Marks New Phase of War
headlines.summary: Russian missiles struck Zakarpattia just 12 hours after Hungarys new prime minister took office, marking what observers call a new phase in the conflict targeting ethnic Hungarian communities in western Ukraine.

headlines.headline: Lebanon Economy to Shrink 7% This Year as War With Israel Rages
headlines.summary: Lebanon's finance minister warned the ongoing conflict with Hezbollah could cost the country $20 billion and shrink GDP by at least 7% this year.

headlines.headline: Japan Exports Rise 14.8% Despite Iran War Supply Disruptions
headlines.summary: Japanese exports beat expectations with an eighth consecutive monthly increase, showing resilience despite major supply chain disruptions from the US-Israel-Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: US, Philippines Near Deal on Economic Security Zone to Counter China
headlines.summary: A senior US official said Washington expects to reach agreement soon on a long-term framework for an economic security zone with the Philippines amid rising tensions with Beijing.

headlines.headline: Full Hormuz Oil Flows Unlikely Before 2027, UAE Oil Chief Says
headlines.summary: The head of UAE state oil firm ADNOC warned that even if the Middle East conflict ended now, full oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz would not return until Q1-Q2 2027 due to infrastructure damage.

headlines.headline: Ukraine develops low-cost interceptor missiles to counter Russian Shahed drones
headlines.summary: Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov announced Ukraine is developing low-cost interceptor missiles with prototypes already in testing. Ukraine plans to increase production tenfold and build stockpiles for fall and winter, following Russian strikes that caused widespread power outages.

headlines.headline: Hungary announces new ban on Ukrainian agricultural imports despite EU law concerns
headlines.summary: Hungary's new agriculture minister announced a fresh ban on Ukrainian produce, saying they will not allow imports that endanger Hungarian farmers. The move potentially breaches EU law, as trade policy is controlled by Brussels, not national capitals.

headlines.headline: China breaks ground on $5 billion aviation complex in UAE despite Iran war risks
headlines.summary: China Railway Construction Corporation has begun building the world's largest aircraft maintenance complex in Dubai, worth over $5 billion. The project signals continued Chinese confidence in Middle East investment despite regional instability from the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: STI Cases Hit Record Levels Across Europe
headlines.summary: Gonorrhoea cases surged 303% since 2015 to over 106,000, while syphilis doubled to 45,557 cases in 2024. Health officials cite widening gaps in testing and prevention, with congenital syphilis in newborns nearly doubling from 2023.

headlines.headline: Barney Frank, Gay Rights Trailblazer and Financial Reformer, Dies at 86
headlines.summary: The longtime Democratic congressman became the first member of Congress to voluntarily come out as gay in 1987 and later the first incumbent lawmaker to marry someone of the same sex. He co-authored the 2010 Dodd-Frank financial reform law.

headlines.headline: Austrian Spy Convicted of Passing Secrets to Wirecard Fugitive
headlines.summary: Egisto Ott, former Austrian intelligence director, found guilty of passing classified information to Wirecard fugitive Jan Marsalek in major espionage case.

headlines.headline: Pentagon official Beijing visit blocked over Taiwan arms package
headlines.summary: China is holding up a proposed visit by Pentagon Under-Secretary Elbridge Colby as Beijing pressures Trump over a $14 billion weapons package for Taiwan.

perspectives.angle: China blocks Pentagon official visit over Taiwan arms package
perspectives.summary: Beijing refused to approve a visit by Pentagon Under-Secretary Elbridge Colby in protest of a $14 billion US weapons package for Taiwan, escalating US-China tensions.

headlines.headline: UK firms suffer widespread activity slide from Iran war fallout
headlines.summary: British companies are experiencing their most widespread drop in activity in over a year due to economic fallout from the Iran war and political uncertainty at home.

headlines.headline: Iran Consolidates Control Over Hormuz Strait With Island Checkpoints
headlines.summary: Reuters investigation reveals Iran has established a multi-tiered clearance system for vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz, combining diplomatic deals, island checkpoints, and sometimes fees, as it consolidates control over the critical waterway.

perspectives.angle: Iran Publishes Map Claiming Armed Forces Control Over Strait of Hormuz
perspectives.summary: Iran has published a map claiming "armed forces oversight" across more than 22,000 sq km of the strategic waterway, stepping up its assertion of control as tensions with the US and Gulf states continue.

headlines.headline: US Preparing Indictment Against Former Cuban President Raúl Castro
headlines.summary: The Justice Department is preparing to seek an indictment against former Cuban President Raúl Castro over the 1996 shootdown of two planes operated by Miami-based exile group Brothers to the Rescue, as US-Cuba tensions escalate following the Venezuela intervention.

headlines.headline: Venezuela Deports Maduro Ally Alex Saab to Face US Prosecution
headlines.summary: Venezuela deported Alex Saab, a close ally of ousted leader Nicolás Maduro who was previously pardoned by Biden in a prisoner swap, marking a major reversal as the new government cooperates with US investigations into the former regime.

headlines.headline: Xi and Putin Strengthen Ties in Beijing Days After Trump Visit
headlines.summary: Chinese leader Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin oversaw signing of over 40 cooperation agreements, emphasizing growing energy trade and strategic alignment in their first meeting since Trump’s diplomatic visit to Beijing.

perspectives.angle: Xi and Putin hail growing China-Russia ties in Beijing meeting
perspectives.summary: Chinese leader Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin oversaw the signing of 40+ cooperation agreements and declared their partnership at its highest level in history, extending a 2001 friendship treaty.

headlines.headline: Syria invited to G7 summit in France as guest nation
headlines.summary: Syria will attend the G7 summit in France next month for the first time, with President Ahmed al-Sharaa representing the country in a major diplomatic breakthrough following years of isolation.

headlines.headline: Wes Streeting pledges wealth tax in Labour leadership challenge
headlines.summary: UK Health Secretary Wes Streeting pledges to introduce a wealth tax if elected Labour leader, directly challenging PM Keir Starmer as pressure mounts on the prime minister.

headlines.headline: Austrian Ex-Intelligence Officer Convicted of Spying for Russia
headlines.summary: Egisto Ott, a former Austrian intelligence official, was sentenced to four years for passing secret information to Russian intelligence and fugitive Wirecard executive Jan Marsalek. The case has revived concerns about Austria as a hub for Russian espionage.

perspectives.angle: Austrian ex-intelligence officer convicted of spying for Russia
perspectives.summary: Egisto Ott, 63, was sentenced to four years for passing classified information to Russian intelligence and Wirecard fugitive Jan Marsalek. The trial revealed Ott copied phones from Austrian interior ministry officials after they fell into the Danube, reviving concerns about Russian espionage in Austria.

headlines.headline: US Surveillance Flights Track Near Cuba Amid Rising Tensions
headlines.summary: Flight-tracking data shows US Navy P-8A Poseidon jets and MQ-4C Triton drones operating within 50 miles of Cuba as Washington maintains pressure on the islands communist government following an effective oil blockade.

headlines.headline: Sierra Leone Receives US Deportees Under Trump Immigration Crackdown
headlines.summary: Nine West African migrants were flown to Sierra Leone as part of Donald Trumps mass deportation policy. The country agreed to accept up to 300 deportees annually, joining other African nations receiving expelled migrants.

headlines.headline: China-Linked Vessel Suspected in Taiwan Submarine Cable Cut
headlines.summary: A submarine cable linking Matsu Island to Taiwan was severed in April, the third incident in four years. Analysis points to a Chinese dredger vessel with maritime militia ties operating under the pretense of a salvage operation. Taiwan faces infrastructure vulnerability as Chinese gray-zone tactics intensify.

headlines.headline: UAE Pipeline Bypassing Hormuz 50% Complete, ADNOC CEO Says
headlines.summary: The UAEs new crude pipeline that bypasses the Strait of Hormuz is about 50% complete, with the head of ADNOC warning that global oil flows may take at least four months to recover after the Iran war ends.

perspectives.angle: UAE Accelerates Second Pipeline to Bypass Strait of Hormuz
perspectives.summary: A UAE company is expediting construction of a second oil pipeline to bypass the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing Iran war disruptions to shipping routes.

perspectives.angle: Gulf states accelerate overland pipeline deals to bypass Hormuz chokepoint
perspectives.summary: Saudi Arabia, UAE, Egypt and Syria are fast-tracking pipeline and logistics agreements to create a Levant-Gulf corridor, fundamentally reshaping regional energy trade routes amid the strait closure.

headlines.headline: Lithuanian Lawmakers Shelter Underground as Drone Violates Airspace
headlines.summary: A drone violated Lithuanian airspace, forcing lawmakers to shelter underground and temporarily suspending flights at Vilnius airport. The incident is the latest in a series of security concerns in the Baltic region.

perspectives.angle: Lithuanian Lawmakers Shelter as Drone Violates Airspace Near Belarus Border
perspectives.summary: Residents of Vilnius were told to take shelter and Lithuanian leaders were moved to safe locations after a drone violated airspace near the Belarus border, highlighting NATO eastern flank security concerns.

headlines.headline: Global Oil Market Faces Supply Crunch Within Months Without Peace Deal
headlines.summary: The oil industry has shown resilience during the Iran conflict, but analysts warn the global market may be only months away from a breaking point if peace talks fail to produce a breakthrough.

headlines.headline: Samsung Averts Strike with Last-Minute Wage Deal, Bonuses Up to $416,000
headlines.summary: Samsung Electronics reached an 11th-hour wage deal with its South Korean union, averting a strike. Shares surged, though concerns remain about the large bonuses included in the agreement.

headlines.headline: Bank of Japan Policymaker Calls for Rate Hike, Cites War-Driven Inflation Risk
headlines.summary: BOJ board member Junko Koeda said the central bank should raise rates at an "appropriate pace," warning that price pressures from the Middle East war could push inflation above the 2% target.

headlines.headline: Iran Reviewing US Peace Proposal as Trump Warns of Further Strikes
headlines.summary: Tehran is reviewing Washington's latest response to a ceasefire proposal, with Trump saying negotiations are in final stages but threatening renewed attacks if no deal is reached.

perspectives.angle: Trump and Netanyahu Held Tense Call on Iran Peace Deal
perspectives.summary: Netanyahu opposed a diplomatic deal to end the Iran war, while Trump said he would continue pursuing negotiations. The call highlights growing U.S.-Israel tensions over how to resolve the conflict.

headlines.headline: Russian Oil and Gas Revenue Surges 39% Due to Iran War Price Rally
headlines.summary: Russia's state oil and gas revenues are projected to rise 39% year-on-year in May to 700 billion roubles, driven by global oil price increases from the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Japan Dismisses Russia-China Claims of Remilitarization
headlines.summary: Tokyo rejected a joint statement by Presidents Xi and Putin accusing Japan of accelerating remilitarization. The dispute comes as Japan strengthens defense ties with South Korea amid China's growing influence.

headlines.headline: Rare Ebola strain spreads rapidly in eastern Congo as health workers struggle
headlines.summary: The Bundibugyo virus, a rare type of Ebola, has spread undetected for weeks in eastern Congo, with 139 suspected deaths and 600 cases. WHO says cases may exceed 1,000, with a vaccine months away amid conflict zones and underfunded health systems.

perspectives.angle: Rare Ebola strain spreads rapidly in eastern Congo as health workers struggle
perspectives.summary: The Bundibugyo virus, a rare type of Ebola, has spread undetected for weeks in eastern Congo, with 139 suspected deaths and 600 cases. WHO says cases may exceed 1,000, with a vaccine months away amid conflict zones and underfunded health systems.

headlines.headline: US military surveillance flights track near Cuba as tensions mount
headlines.summary: BBC Verify analysis shows at least five US Navy P-8A Poseidon jets and three MQ-4C Triton drones have operated near Cuba since May 11, with flights as close as 50 miles from the island. Experts say the visible flight transponders are likely a deliberate signal to enforce the oil blockade and deter Venezuela.

headlines.headline: Xi Jinping May Visit North Korea as Early as Next Week
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping may visit North Korea as early as next week in what would be his first trip to the country in seven years. The visit comes amid efforts to strengthen bilateral ties and potential mediation between Trump and Kim Jong Un.

perspectives.angle: Xi Jinping May Visit North Korea Next Week to Strengthen Ties
perspectives.summary: Chinese President expected to travel to Pyongyang as early as next week to meet Kim Jong-un in bid to bring North Korea closer into China fold amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Xi and Putin Conclude Beijing Summit Without Finalizing Gas Pipeline Deal
headlines.summary: Chinese and Russian leaders wrapped up talks in Beijing with an "understanding" on the long-stalled Power of Siberia 2 gas pipeline but no final agreement. The summit came days after Xi hosted Trump, highlighting China's balancing act between major powers.

headlines.headline: Xi Warned Trump That Taiwan Differences Could Lead to Conflict
headlines.summary: During their summit, Chinese President Xi Jinping warned Trump that differences over Taiwan could lead to conflict between the two powers. The discussion highlights Taiwan as a critical flashpoint in US-China relations.

headlines.headline: Hezbollah Conducts 24 Operations Against Israeli Forces in 24 Hours
headlines.summary: The group claims attacks included drone swarms, Merkava tank strikes, and direct confrontations with Israeli troops, escalating border conflict intensity.

headlines.headline: Putin's Trolls Weakening Merkel's Successor to Boost Russia-Friendly Far Right
headlines.summary: As German Chancellor Merz struggles domestically and the AfD rises ahead of key elections, Russian disinformation campaigns are intensifying to influence German politics.

headlines.headline: Australia to Begin Construction on Major Rare Earth Project in September
headlines.summary: Arafura Resources, backed by Gina Rinehart and the federal government, will build Australia's first ore-to-oxide rare earth processing facility, reducing Western dependence on Chinese supply chains.

headlines.headline: Australian Court Upholds $465,000 Fine Against Musk's X Over Child Safety
headlines.summary: X Corp admitted violating Australian law by failing to provide information about child protection measures, ending a three-year legal battle with the eSafety commissioner.

headlines.headline: US Racing to Develop Ebola Treatment for Congo Outbreak
headlines.summary: The US government is working with a small biotech firm on an experimental treatment for the Bundibugyo Ebola strain causing the deadly outbreak in Congo, for which no approved vaccine exists.

perspectives.angle: Angry Mob Burns Ebola Treatment Center in DR Congo
perspectives.summary: Locals set fire to a treatment facility in Rwampara after being barred from retrieving a suspected Ebola victim's body. The outbreak has killed 160 people and spread to South Kivu province, with no vaccine available for the Bundibugyo strain.

headlines.headline: Xi and Putin Wrap Beijing Talks with No Deal on Key Gas Pipeline
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin left Beijing with no breakthrough on a major natural gas pipeline project, though the two leaders signed over 40 cooperation agreements and jointly criticized US policies.

headlines.headline: UK Inflation Cools More Than Expected in April
headlines.summary: British inflation fell more than expected in April, but the slowdown offers only temporary relief as global costs from the Iran war are expected to hit households harder later this year.

headlines.headline: US Indicts Former Cuban President Raúl Castro Over 1996 Plane Shootdown
headlines.summary: Federal prosecutors announced charges against former Cuban President Raúl Castro for his role in the 1996 downing of civilian planes operated by Miami-based exiles, escalating US pressure on Cuba. Trump said no escalation is expected.

perspectives.angle: US Charges Former Cuban Leader Raúl Castro with Murder Over 1996 Plane Shootdown
perspectives.summary: The US Justice Department charged the 94-year-old former Cuban president with conspiracy to kill US nationals and destruction of aircraft over the 1996 downing of two planes that killed four people, including three Americans. Cuba called the charges a political maneuver.

headlines.headline: NATO Chief Rutte Says US Troop Withdrawal From Europe Will Not Harm Defense
headlines.summary: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte stated that planned US troop withdrawals from Europe will unfold gradually in a structured way and will not impact NATO defense plans, as Europe moves to strengthen its own defense capabilities.

perspectives.angle: NATO Military Chief Says No More US Troop Drawdowns Expected From Europe
perspectives.summary: NATO's top military officer said he doesn't expect additional US troop reductions from Europe beyond the 5,000 already announced by Trump. The announcement comes amid European concerns about US commitment to the alliance.

perspectives.angle: Rutte dismisses US military drawdown in Europe as expected
perspectives.summary: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said reduced US military presence in Europe is normal as Trump administration shifts focus. European allies debate scaling up defense capabilities independently.

perspectives.angle: Europe Considers Secret Military Alliance to Replace NATO Dependence
perspectives.summary: The Economist reports European officials are quietly developing contingency plans for collective defense without US involvement, amid growing concerns about American commitment to the alliance.

headlines.headline: Taiwan president warns foreign forces cannot decide island future
headlines.summary: President Lai Ching-te stated that foreign powers cannot determine Taiwan future following Trump comments on arms sales to the island. China continues pressure campaign on the self-governing democracy.

headlines.headline: Norway offshore oil output beats forecasts by 6.7%
headlines.summary: Norwegian offshore production reached 2.158 million barrels per day in April, exceeding official forecasts by 6.7%. The surge highlights North Sea energy resilience amid global supply concerns.

headlines.headline: UAE departure from OPEC reshapes global oil dynamics
headlines.summary: UAE exit from OPEC raises questions about future oil pricing and supply coordination. The move could shift production strategy for one of the worlds largest oil exporters.

headlines.headline: Iran warns renewed US-Israel attack would expand conflict beyond region
headlines.summary: IRGC says Tehran has not yet used all of its capabilities against adversaries. Warning comes amid fragile ceasefire and ongoing tensions in the Strait of Hormuz.

perspectives.angle: IRGC Warns War Will Spread Far Beyond Region If US-Israel Resume Attacks
perspectives.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guards warned that renewed US-Israeli attacks would trigger a conflict spreading "far beyond the region." The statement comes as Trump set a deadline of several days for a deal, threatening to resume strikes if no agreement is reached.

headlines.headline: US charges Raúl Castro with murder over 1996 plane shootdown
headlines.summary: The Justice Department unsealed an indictment charging former Cuban President Raúl Castro with murder for the 1996 downing of two civilian planes operated by Cuban exiles, amid escalating US pressure on Cuba.

perspectives.angle: US Indicts Former Cuban Leader Raúl Castro Over 1996 Plane Shootdown
perspectives.summary: The US charged the 94-year-old former president with conspiracy to kill US nationals and murder over the downing of two Brothers to the Rescue aircraft that killed four people, including three Americans. Castro faces potential life imprisonment or death penalty if convicted.

headlines.headline: UK reports dangerous Russian jet intercept over Black Sea
headlines.summary: Britain defense ministry said two Russian jets dangerously intercepted a RAF surveillance plane over the Black Sea last month, raising concerns about potential NATO-Russia escalation.

headlines.headline: Iran Displays Weapons at Public Events as Tehran Readies for Potential War Resumption
headlines.summary: Iranian Revolutionary Guard members are conducting public weapons training in Tehran, with ballistic missiles displayed at mass weddings. The demonstrations aim to project strength and motivate hardliners amid economic hardship and war uncertainty.

headlines.headline: Iran Claims IRGC Coordinated Transit of 26 Vessels Through Hormuz in 24 Hours
headlines.summary: Iran says its Revolutionary Guard is controlling traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, publishing a new map marking a controlled maritime zone. The FAO warned the blockade could trigger a severe global food price crisis within 6-12 months.

headlines.headline: Developed Asia draws private capital as investors pivot away from China
headlines.summary: Japan is leading a shift in private capital allocation as LPs prioritize exits, stronger governance, and proven returns, with Australia and Korea also gaining attention from investors seeking alternatives to China exposure.

headlines.headline: Russia has finally turned its back on Europe, Berliner Zeitung reports
headlines.summary: German newspaper Berliner Zeitung writes that Russia has reoriented itself to China, with EU-Russia trade shrinking from nearly $400 billion in 2013 to just $64 billion by 2025, an 80% decline since 2021.

headlines.headline: Erdogan tells Trump Iran issues can be resolved as Turkey mediates
headlines.summary: Turkish President Erdogan told Trump he welcomed the ceasefire extension with Iran and believes contested issues can be resolved. Turkey has been passing messages between Washington, Tehran, and mediators Pakistan.

perspectives.angle: Turkey's Erdogan Tells Trump Iran Issues Can Be Resolved
perspectives.summary: Turkish President Erdogan told Trump in a call he welcomed the extension of a ceasefire and believes issues with Iran can be resolved, positioning Turkey as a potential mediator.

headlines.headline: Turkey's parliament unites to demand US extradite FETO leader Gulen
headlines.summary: Turkey's governing and opposition parties issued a joint declaration calling for the extradition of Fetullah Gulen, the US-based leader of FETO, which Turkey accuses of orchestrating the 2016 coup attempt that killed 251 people.

headlines.headline: US military surveillance flights track movements near Cuba amid tensions
headlines.summary: BBC Verify analysis shows US Navy P-8A Poseidon jets and MQ-4C Triton drones have been operating near Cuba since May 11, with some flights coming within 50 miles of the island. The public nature of the flights signals US intent to enforce the oil blockade and pressure Havana.

headlines.headline: Russian jets dangerously intercept British spy plane over Black Sea
headlines.summary: The UK Defence Ministry said two Russian Su-35 and Su-27 aircraft repeatedly intercepted an unarmed RAF Rivet Joint surveillance aircraft in international airspace in April, coming within six metres of the plane. Britain called it the most dangerous Russian action against a UK aircraft since 2022.

headlines.headline: Iran reopens stock market after three-month war closure
headlines.summary: Tehran Stock Exchange resumed trading with restrictions after being shut since February. About 36% of companies remain suspended due to war damage, while inflation exceeds 70% and the rial continues to slide against the dollar.

headlines.headline: US Doctor Exposed to Ebola Transferred to Czech Hospital from Uganda
headlines.summary: A US doctor who came into contact with an Ebola patient will be transferred from Uganda to a hospital in Prague for monitoring, Czech health officials said.

headlines.headline: American Ebola Patient in Stable Condition in German Hospital
headlines.summary: A US citizen who contracted Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo is being treated in Germany and is in stable condition, the CDC said, as the rare Bundibugyo strain outbreak continues.

headlines.headline: Iranian public receives gun training as war fears mount
headlines.summary: Revolutionary Guard members are teaching civilians to handle assault rifles in Tehran, with weapons displays and missiles featured at public events amid Trump threats to restart the war.

perspectives.angle: Iranian civilians receive assault rifle training amid war fears
perspectives.summary: Groups of Iranians are learning to use AK-47 rifles from Revolutionary Guard instructors in central Tehran as domestic preparedness efforts intensify.

headlines.headline: US envoy pushes to reestablish military presence in Greenland
headlines.summary: US special envoy Jeff Landry says Washington needs to put its footprint back on Greenland, with plans for three new bases as Trump pursues control of the Arctic territory.

headlines.headline: South Lebanon cultural heritage destroyed amid Israeli buffer zone creation
headlines.summary: Lebanese activists document widespread demolition of religious structures and historic sites as Israel establishes a forward defence zone encompassing 55 villages since April ceasefire.

headlines.headline: Bank of England Has Time to Gauge Iran War Economic Impact
headlines.summary: A rise in market interest rates since the start of the Iran war has given the Bank of England more time to assess the economic impact of the conflict, according to Governor Andrew Bailey.

perspectives.angle: ASEAN Manufacturers Shed Jobs as Iran War Impact Deepens
perspectives.summary: Manufacturers across Southeast Asia are reporting company closures and job cuts as supply chain disruptions from the Iran conflict bite. Key industries in Malaysia, Philippines and Vietnam face mounting pressure from rising costs and logistics bottlenecks.

headlines.headline: Ebola Outbreak Response Hindered by US Aid Cuts Under Trump
headlines.summary: Aid cuts by the Trump administration have shut down crucial disease surveillance networks and medical supply chains in East Africa, potentially hampering efforts to contain the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

headlines.headline: Saudi Arabia Crude Exports Drop to Record Low in March
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia's crude oil exports fell to a record low of 4.974 million barrels per day in March, the lowest level since data collection began in 2002, according to JODI data.

headlines.headline: US indicts former Cuban president Raúl Castro over 1996 plane downing
headlines.summary: The US Justice Department charged the 94-year-old former Cuban leader with conspiracy to kill US nationals and murder for the 1996 shooting down of two humanitarian planes that killed four people.

headlines.headline: US Marines Test Fire Rocket System Near Mount Fuji in Rapid Drill
headlines.summary: US Marines test fired a dozen rockets from a mobile launcher at a range in Japan's foothills near Mount Fuji, demonstrating "shoot and scoot" capabilities.

headlines.headline: Germany Plans 260,000-Strong Bundeswehr as US Pullout Accelerates European Rearmament
headlines.summary: Defense Minister Boris Pistorius called for expanding the Bundeswehr to 260,000 soldiers, reversing 30 years of force reductions as the US military withdrawal from Europe spurs unprecedented defense buildup.

headlines.headline: Zelensky Marks Seven Years in Office as War Defines Legacy
headlines.summary: Seven years after taking office as a showman turned wartime leader, Zelensky's legacy is now inextricably tied to the ongoing conflict with Russia.

headlines.headline: Russia conducts nuclear warhead transport exercise amid tensions with West
headlines.summary: Russian forces demonstrated movement of nuclear warheads in a three-day exercise, showcasing capability at a time when Moscow describes itself in an existential struggle with the West over Ukraine.

headlines.headline: Kremlin calls Lithuanian minister Kaliningrad comments verging on insanity
headlines.summary: Russia sharply criticized Lithuania after its foreign minister said NATO should demonstrate capability to penetrate the Russian exclave of Kaliningrad, calling the remarks "verging on insanity."

perspectives.angle: Kremlin Slams Lithuania Minister Comments on Kaliningrad as Insane
perspectives.summary: The Kremlin called remarks by Lithuanias foreign minister "verging on insanity" after he said NATO should show Moscow it was capable of penetrating the Russian exclave of Kaliningrad.

headlines.headline: Iran Used Triangular Coercion in Gulf Strategy
headlines.summary: Outmatched militarily, Iran attacked Gulf states and closed the Strait of Hormuz using asymmetric tactics, revealing a long-term U.S. strategic vulnerability.

headlines.headline: Iran Warns of War Beyond Gulf Region if US Attacks Again
headlines.summary: Iran Revolutionary Guards threatens retaliation in places beyond the region if the U.S. or Israel launch new attacks, as Trump says he was an hour away from striking.

perspectives.angle: Iran threatens to expand war beyond Middle East if US launches new attacks
perspectives.summary: The IRGC warned that any new US or Israeli strikes on Tehran would trigger retaliation in places we cannot even imagine, as diplomatic efforts to finalize a ceasefire deal continue.

headlines.headline: Chinese investment in Europe surges to 7-year high despite trade tensions
headlines.summary: Chinese FDI in Europe hit a seven-year peak, but analysts warn the pipeline of new projects is drying up as investors face pressure from both Beijing and European capitals.

headlines.headline: Over 160 oil tankers stranded in Gulf as Iran tightens Hormuz grip
headlines.summary: More than 160 tankers carrying approximately 160 million barrels of oil remain stuck as Iran consolidates control over the Strait of Hormuz, creating one of the worst shipping bottlenecks in decades.

perspectives.angle: China Turns to Russian Oil as Gulf Supply Drops Amid Hormuz Crisis
perspectives.summary: Chinese customs data shows Russian crude shipments to China rose 11.3% year-on-year in April as Gulf imports plunged during the Strait of Hormuz crisis. However, the discount on Russian oil has shrunk significantly due to sanctions waivers boosting global demand for Russian barrels.

headlines.headline: Russia arming missile warheads with depleted uranium, Ukrainian intelligence says
headlines.summary: Ukraines SBU security service found depleted uranium projectiles in R-60 missile warheads, raising concerns about Russias battlefield tactics and potential long-term contamination.

headlines.headline: Iran consolidates Strait of Hormuz control with island checkpoints and passage fees
headlines.summary: A Reuters investigation reveals Iran has established a multi-tiered system for clearing vessels through the Strait of Hormuz, including government-to-government arrangements and transit fees, as nations seek to replenish dwindling energy supplies throttled by the war.

headlines.headline: Rubio heads to NATO meeting amid European concerns over US reliability
headlines.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will attend NATO foreign ministers meeting in Sweden this week, where US plans to reduce troop levels in Europe and Trump policy inconsistency have raised anxiety about American commitment to the alliance.

headlines.headline: China and US reach limited trade agreements on Boeing jets and beef
headlines.summary: China agreed to purchase 200 Boeing aircraft and resume imports of some US beef products, marking one of the clearest signs yet of easing trade tensions between the two countries following Trump Beijing summit.

headlines.headline: EU reaches deal on US trade pact ahead of Trump tariff deadline
headlines.summary: EU lawmakers and member states reached agreement to implement the trade pact with the United States, ending months of wrangling and likely averting higher US tariffs threatened by President Trump for July 4.

perspectives.angle: EU Strikes Deal on US Trade Pact Ahead of Trump Tariff Deadline
perspectives.summary: The European Parliament and Council reached a late-night provisional agreement to remove import duties on US goods, keeping the bloc on track to meet the July 4 deadline and avoid higher US tariffs on European goods.

headlines.headline: China Confirms 200 Boeing Jet Purchase After Trump-Xi Summit
headlines.summary: China announced it will buy 200 Boeing aircraft and seek an extension of the US-China tariff truce through November, signalling a stabilization in relations between the world's two largest economies following last week's Beijing summit.

headlines.headline: EU Strikes Deal to Finalize US Trade Agreement, Avert Tariffs
headlines.summary: The European Union reached provisional agreement on legislation to remove import duties on US goods, a key part of a trade deal with Washington that would prevent higher US tariffs on EU products.

headlines.headline: Trump Approval Drops to 35% as Republican Support Softens
headlines.summary: A new Reuters/Ipsos poll shows President Donald Trump's approval rating falling to 35%, with softening support among Republicans as the administration navigates the Iran crisis and domestic challenges.

perspectives.angle: Trump Approval Drops to 35% Amid Softening Republican Support
perspectives.summary: President Trump's approval rating fell to 35% in a Reuters/Ipsos poll, just above the 34% low of his presidency seen last month, as Republican support continues to soften.

headlines.headline: Fed Minutes Reveal Deep Divisions Over Rate Policy
headlines.summary: The Federal Reserve's latest meeting minutes reveal significant disagreement among policymakers on interest rate direction and inflation severity, exposing the most divided Fed in a generation.

headlines.headline: Israeli Minister Calls Turkey an Enemy State
headlines.summary: Israels culture minister said Turkey should be treated as an enemy state, warning it could become the next Iran or Qatar. The comments further strain already tense Israel-Turkey relations.

headlines.headline: Baltic Lawmakers Press EU for Firm Support Against Russian Strike Threats
headlines.summary: Lithuanian and Baltic lawmakers are urging the EU to provide firm backing as Moscow threatens strikes in response to comments about attacking the Russian exclave of Kaliningrad.

headlines.headline: UK Eases Russian Oil Sanctions as Fuel Prices Soar
headlines.summary: Britain has waived sanctions on diesel and jet fuel processed from Russian crude in third countries, citing energy supply security as domestic fuel prices continue to rise.

headlines.headline: IDF Brigade Commander Seriously Wounded in Lebanon Drone Strike
headlines.summary: The commander of the IDF's 401st Armored Brigade was seriously wounded when an explosive-laden drone struck his position in south Lebanon, amid ongoing cross-border hostilities.

headlines.headline: Lithuania evacuates leaders after drone alert near Vilnius airport
headlines.summary: Lithuanian president and prime minister were moved to safety after a suspected drone was detected near Vilnius airport, highlighting security concerns on NATO eastern flank amid tensions with Russia and Belarus.

headlines.headline: Rubio addresses Cuban people in video offering new US relationship
headlines.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio directly addressed Cubans in a video message, offering $100 million in aid and blaming former leader Raúl Castro for the country's shortages of electricity, food and fuel, while urging a new chapter in US-Cuba relations.

headlines.headline: Trump says Iran negotiations in final stages amid Hormuz tensions
headlines.summary: President Trump announced that negotiations with Iran are in their final stages while warning of further attacks if Tehran fails to reach a deal, as tankers exit the Strait of Hormuz and oil markets watch closely.

perspectives.angle: Trump Calls Off Iran Attack Amid "Serious Negotiations"
perspectives.summary: The president postponed planned strikes on Iran at the request of Gulf Arab states, claiming serious negotiations are underway. Iran shows no signs of budging on key demands despite threats.

headlines.headline: Rubio to visit Sweden for NATO meeting on burden sharing then India
headlines.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will attend a NATO foreign ministers meeting in Sweden to discuss greater burden sharing by allies, before traveling to India as the Trump administration reviews its global military commitments.

perspectives.angle: Rubio Heads to NATO Meeting, First Visit to India
perspectives.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will meet NATO foreign ministers in Sweden on Friday to discuss defense burden-sharing, then make his first visit to India. The trip follows US troop withdrawal announcements and strained ties with India over trade and diplomatic issues.

headlines.headline: Rubio Offers Cuba New Relationship with $100 Million in Aid
headlines.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio offered to forge a new relationship with Cuba in a video message, proposing $100 million in aid and blaming Cuban leaders for shortages of electricity, food and fuel.

headlines.headline: UK Inflation Cools More Than Expected in April
headlines.summary: British inflation fell more than expected in April, but the slowdown masks a tough outlook for households as Iran war-related costs are set to hit consumers harder later this year.

headlines.headline: Commerzbank Staff and Executives Resist UniCredit Takeover Bid
headlines.summary: Employees, executives and some investors criticized UniCredit takeover attempt at the German banks shareholder meeting, maintaining resistance to the cross-border deal.

headlines.headline: Qatar Airways Profit Falls 7% as Iran War Hits Global Network
headlines.summary: State-owned Qatar Airways posted a more than 7% decline in annual net profit but flagged progress in rebuilding its global schedule as it navigates the impact of the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Somaliland to open embassy in Jerusalem after Israel becomes first to recognize breakaway state
headlines.summary: The self-declared republic announced it will establish an embassy in Jerusalem following Israel's recognition in December 2025, with Israel planning to reciprocate by opening an embassy in Hargeisa.

headlines.headline: US seizes Iran-linked oil tanker in Indian Ocean carrying over one million barrels
headlines.summary: The US seized a sanctioned Iranian oil tanker in the Indian Ocean, the Wall Street Journal reported. The vessel had loaded crude at Iran's Kharg Island in February.

headlines.headline: Handful of tankers still crossing Hormuz despite conflict, using transponder tricks
headlines.summary: About 19 non-Iranian tankers have successfully transited the Strait of Hormuz since March, with Greek firm Dynacom using tactics like disabling transponders and sailing at night to avoid attacks.

headlines.headline: US expected to announce criminal charges against Cuba's Raul Castro
headlines.summary: The Trump administration is expected to announce criminal charges against former Cuban President Raul Castro as part of an intensified pressure campaign against the communist government.

perspectives.angle: China Condemns US Charges Against Raul Castro, Vows Support for Cuba
perspectives.summary: Beijing criticized US "big stick" diplomacy after Washington indicted former Cuban leader Raul Castro on murder charges, expressing solidarity with Havana and defending Cuba sovereignty.

headlines.headline: Xi and Putin signal united front against Washington in Beijing talks
headlines.summary: Chinese leader Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin demonstrated a united front against the US during their Beijing meeting, deepening strategic cooperation between the two powers amid tensions with Washington.

headlines.headline: Indonesia unveils plan to centralise control of commodity exports
headlines.summary: Indonesia announced a sweeping plan to centralise exports of key commodities like palm oil and coal through a state agency, aiming to boost government revenue through tighter control of natural resource sales.

headlines.headline: US Senate Advances Resolution to Limit Trump's Iran War Powers
headlines.summary: The US Senate moved forward with a bipartisan resolution requiring congressional approval before extending military action against Iran, a significant check on presidential war-making authority.

perspectives.angle: Senate advances bill to end Iran war as Republican Cassidy flips
perspectives.summary: The Senate voted 50-47 to advance legislation forcing President Trump to withdraw from the Iran conflict, with Sen. Bill Cassidy joining Democrats after his primary election loss.

headlines.headline: Xi and Putin Unite Against US on Nuclear and Security Issues
headlines.summary: Chinese and Russian leaders met in Beijing days after Trump's visit, stressing their "unyielding" ties and criticizing US security policies including the "Golden Dome" missile defense program.
